meaning: n.巫师,医师,(印地安人的)仪式vi.举行仪式,行巫术vt.用巫术医治
examples: Every year my father would call a family powwow to discuss where we were going on holiday.
But a powwow wasn't always a meeting.
The author went to a powwow on the Fort Peck Indian Reservation outside Wolf Point, Mont.
In the colorful regalia he might wear to a powwow, Mr. Box danced to the rhythm of hums, drums and an eagle-bone whistle.
In particular, Paulson's powwow in December was reminiscent of one of President Herbert Hoover's moves in the fall of 1929, following the stock market crash.
